title_en: The Panama Canal is growing more important
title_zh: 巴拿马运河正变得更加重要
rubric_en: And the challenges to it are getting more acute
rubric_zh: 而它面临的挑战也愈发严峻

paragraphs.en: When Donald Trump’s war with Iran first closed the Strait of Hormuz this year, the effects were felt swiftly on the other side of the world. Demand for passage through the Panama Canal, the 80km waterway linking the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, shot up to near its maximum capacity of 36 to 40 transits a day. The number of ships carrying fuel through the canal rose sharply as countries turned to American energy. The number of liquefied-natural-gas (LNG) cargoes transiting almost doubled in April, compared with the same month last year.
paragraphs.zh: 今年，唐纳德·特朗普与伊朗的战争首次导致霍尔木兹海峡关闭时，世界另一端很快就感受到了影响。巴拿马运河这条连接大西洋和太平洋、全长80公里的水道，通行需求飙升，接近每天36至40次过境的最大运力。随着各国转向美国能源，经由运河运输燃料的船只数量大幅增加。4月份通过运河的液化天然气（LNG）货运量与去年同期相比几乎翻了一番。

paragraphs.en: The Panama Canal carries just 5-6% of world maritime trade. That is less than some other chokepoints, such as the Strait of Malacca near Singapore. Yet its importance is growing, both because of the Middle East war and because of enduring changes to flows of goods and energy. This could be a boon to the country of 4.6m people that hosts it, but only if it can avoid becoming a pawn in the rivalry between China and the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 巴拿马运河承载的海上贸易量仅占全球的5-6%。这低于其他一些咽喉要道，例如新加坡附近的马六甲海峡。然而，由于中东战争以及货物和能源流向的持久变化，它的重要性正在上升。对拥有460万人口、坐拥这条运河的巴拿马来说，这可能是一大利好，但前提是它能避免成为中国与美国竞争中的棋子。

paragraphs.en: The goods that pass along the canal run the gamut, from toys to cars, fertilisers to fruit. But its significance as a route for energy shipments is especially striking, largely due to the United States’ rise as an exporter of LNG and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). The canal built the infrastructure needed to accept this traffic in 2016. It currently moves around 1m barrels of LPG a day, much of it travelling from the Gulf Coast to petrochemical plants in Asia.
paragraphs.zh: 通过运河的货物种类繁多，从玩具到汽车，从化肥到水果，应有尽有。但它作为能源运输路线的重要性尤其引人注目，这在很大程度上是因为美国崛起为液化天然气和液化石油气（LPG）出口国。2016年，运河建成了接纳这类运输所需的基础设施。目前，它每天运输约100万桶液化石油气，其中大部分从美国墨西哥湾沿岸运往亚洲的石化工厂。

paragraphs.en: The Economist met Ricaurte Vásquez Morales, the canal’s administrator, in a control room overlooking the waterway. He says the canal authority has launched $8.5bn worth of projects on his watch. The boldest is a pipeline to carry LpG across the isthmus, which could raise its energy capacity by up to 2.5m barrels a day. In addition, two new port terminals will expand container capacity. The aim, he says, is to make Panama not merely a passage between oceans but a broader market for trade.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》在一间俯瞰运河的控制室里见到了运河管理局局长里考尔特·巴斯克斯·莫拉莱斯。他说，在他任内，运河管理局已启动价值85亿美元的项目。其中最大胆的是一条把液化石油气输送穿越地峡的管道，这可能使其能源运输能力每天最多增加250万桶。此外，两个新的港口码头将扩大集装箱运力。他说，目标是让巴拿马不仅仅成为两大洋之间的通道，而是成为更广泛的贸易市场。

paragraphs.en: The extra traffic because of the war in the Gulf may help pay for that. The auctioned cost of passage through the canal (some passages are pre-booked but the most in-demand slots are auctioned) nearly tripled from a pre-Hormuz average of $135,000-140,000 to about $385,000-425,000 in April and May. One company paid $4m for a single slot. In the fiscal year to September 2025 the canal put almost $3bn into Panama’s treasury, a record amount that accounts for over a fifth of state revenue.
paragraphs.zh: 海湾战争带来的额外交通量可能有助于支付这些项目的费用。运河通行的拍卖价格（部分通行名额可提前预订，但最抢手的时段会进行拍卖）从霍尔木兹海峡关闭前平均13.5万至14万美元，几乎涨了两倍，在4月和5月达到约38.5万至42.5万美元。一家公司为单个通行时段支付了400万美元。在截至2025年9月的财政年度，运河为巴拿马国库贡献了近30亿美元，创下纪录，占国家收入的五分之一以上。

paragraphs.en: Mr Vásquez is aware that the canal’s current good fortune may prove to be “a blip”. But some of it may last. It is far from clear when Hormuz will be fully open, given the resumption of strikes between Iran and America in recent weeks. Even if it does open, trade patterns will not revert quite to how they were before, reckon researchers at Goldman Sachs, a bank.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生意识到，运河眼下的好运可能只是“昙花一现”。但其中一部分影响也许会持续。鉴于最近几周伊朗与美国之间再次相互打击，霍尔木兹海峡何时能够完全开放还远不明朗。高盛这家银行的研究人员认为，即便它重新开放，贸易格局也不会完全回到从前。

paragraphs.en: Yet Mr Vásquez—and his designated successor, Ilya Espino de Marotta, who is due to take over in October—face two big challenges. One is the canal’s vulnerability to climate change. Its locks are supplied by lakes which depend on rainfall for replenishment. A severe drought in 2023-24 forced the authority to cut daily transits to as few as 18. It also caused many LNG carriers to switch to a route around the Cape of Good Hope, despite it taking longer; many of them have not yet returned to the canal, says Francis Zeimetz of Panama’s Maritime Chamber. This year’s El Niño could cause similar disruption.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，巴斯克斯先生以及他指定的继任者伊利娅·埃斯皮诺·德·马罗塔面临两大挑战；后者预计将在10月接任。其一是运河容易受到气候变化影响。运河船闸由湖泊供水，而湖泊依靠降雨补给。2023-24年的严重干旱迫使管理局将每日通行次数削减至最低18次。巴拿马海事商会的弗朗西斯·蔡梅茨说，这也导致许多液化天然气运输船改走绕行好望角的路线，尽管这条路线耗时更长；其中许多船至今尚未回到巴拿马运河。今年的厄尔尼诺现象可能造成类似干扰。

paragraphs.en: Hence another big project that is at last getting under way: a reservoir on the Indio river, damming it to secure the canal’s water supply. The decades-old plan languished because it was costly and controversial. It will displace some 2,000 people. Finally approved in 2025, works are due to start next year and should be completed by 2032. Mr Vásquez says it will supply enough water for roughly 11.5 extra transits a day in a dry year, ensuring the canal’s operation for the next half-century.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，另一个大型项目终于开始推进：在因迪奥河上修建水库，通过筑坝保障运河供水。这个已有数十年历史的计划曾因成本高昂且争议不断而搁置。它将导致约2,000人迁离。该项目于2025年最终获批，工程计划明年启动，并应在2032年前完成。巴斯克斯先生说，在干旱年份，它将提供足够水量，使每天通行次数大约增加11.5次，确保运河未来半个世纪的运营。

paragraphs.en: The most pressing problem, however, is geopolitics. Panama has recently found itself in the middle of a rivalry between the United States and China. Since returning to the White House in January 2025, Mr Trump has repeatedly threatened to “take back” the Panama Canal, which the United States built and operated from 1914 until it handed it to Panama to run in 1999. It seems like bluster, but it is a theme he returns to. This month he again ruminated on it in two speeches. The attention is a shock after years when Panama appeared to fall off the maps in Washington. The United States did not post an ambassador to Panama between 2018 and 2022.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，最紧迫的问题是地缘政治。巴拿马最近发现自己处在美国和中国竞争的夹缝中。自2025年1月重返白宫以来，特朗普先生多次威胁要“收回”巴拿马运河。美国从1914年起修建并运营这条运河，直到1999年才把它交给巴拿马管理。这听起来像是虚张声势，但却是他反复提起的主题。本月，他又在两次演讲中琢磨起这件事。对巴拿马来说，这种关注令人震惊，因为多年来它似乎已从华盛顿的地图上消失。2018年至2022年间，美国没有向巴拿马派驻大使。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump is worried about the security of America’s supply chains (more than 70% of traffic in the Panama Canal is going to or coming from the United States). In 2025 his administration opened an investigation into global chokepoints. His interest also results from his revival of the 19th-century Monroe Doctrine, which holds the Americas to be the backyard of the United States and considers outside powers unwelcome. He has repeatedly said that China is “running” the canal.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生担心美国供应链的安全（巴拿马运河超过70%的交通量是驶往美国或来自美国）。2025年，他的政府对全球咽喉要道展开调查。他的兴趣也源于他重新唤起19世纪的门罗主义；该主义认为美洲是美国的后院，并认为外部势力不受欢迎。他多次声称中国正在“运营”这条运河。

paragraphs.en: Foreign firms do not run the canal, but some hold concessions in the canal’s hinterland, notes Mr Vásquez. Alonso Illueca, a Panamanian lawyer, notes that China’s footprint has increased rapidly in Panama in recent years.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生指出，外国公司并不运营运河，但有一些公司在运河腹地持有特许经营权。巴拿马律师阿隆索·伊柳埃卡指出，近年来中国在巴拿马的存在迅速扩大。

paragraphs.en: In 1996 the Panama Ports Company, a subsidiary of the Hong Kong-based conglomerate CK Hutchison, won concessions to run the ports of Balboa, at the Pacific end of the canal, and Cristóbal, at the Atlantic end. Other Chinese firms clustered around the waterway. Panama too often treats strategic assets as ordinary holdings that may be bought and sold, says Mr Illueca. Some also allege that Chinese companies, bound more by the needs of the Chinese Communist Party than rule of law, have found it easier than their American rivals to strike deals in a country that is notorious for corruption.
paragraphs.zh: 1996年，总部位于香港的企业集团长江和记旗下的巴拿马港口公司获得特许经营权，经营运河太平洋端的巴尔博亚港和大西洋端的克里斯托瓦尔港。其他中国企业也聚集在这条水道周围。伊柳埃卡先生说，巴拿马太常把战略资产当作可买可卖的普通持有资产。也有人指称，中国企业受中国共产党的需求约束多于受法治约束，因此在这个以腐败著称的国家，比美国竞争对手更容易达成交易。

paragraphs.en: American pressure has already had an impact. In January Panama’s Supreme Court ruled that the Panama Ports Company’s contract was unconstitutional. José Raúl Mulino, Panama’s president, handed the ports to two European firms until new contracts can be tendered. Last year Panama also left the Belt and Road Initiative, China’s international infrastructure-investment scheme, shortly after Mr Trump returned to the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的压力已经产生影响。1月，巴拿马最高法院裁定巴拿马港口公司的合同违宪。巴拿马总统何塞·劳尔·穆利诺将这些港口交给两家欧洲公司管理，直到新合同可以招标。去年，在特朗普先生重返白宫后不久，巴拿马也退出了中国的国际基础设施投资计划“一带一路”倡议。

paragraphs.en: Mr Vásquez notes that the canal never closed during the pandemic; nor has it during drought or geopolitical turmoil. Panama has done a “tremendous job” running it, says Louis Sola, who used to head the Federal Maritime Commission, an independent regulator in Washington. But Panama and the canal’s administrator will have a hard time navigating the next few years, stuck between an American president who wants more control and a Chinese government that will make life hard for those who hurt its interests.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生指出，运河在疫情期间从未关闭；在干旱或地缘政治动荡期间也没有关闭。曾领导美国联邦海事委员会这一华盛顿独立监管机构的路易斯·索拉说，巴拿马在运营运河方面做得“非常出色”。但在未来几年，巴拿马和运河管理者将很难周旋：一边是想要更多控制权的美国总统，另一边是会让损害其利益者日子不好过的中国政府。

paragraphs.en: China has started to retaliate for Panama’s moves to appease Mr Trump. In March, 91 of 123 ships detained at Chinese ports were Panama-flagged; that figure climbed higher in April to 136 vessels. American officials say the detentions show no sign of ending. China is “trying to make Panama hurt”, says Mr Illueca. Asked what sums up his seven years at the helm, Mr Vásquez says “volatility”. His successor can expect more of that. ■
paragraphs.zh: 中国已经开始报复巴拿马为安抚特朗普先生而采取的举措。3月，在中国港口被扣留的123艘船中，有91艘悬挂巴拿马旗；4月这一数字进一步升至136艘。美国官员说，这些扣留看不到结束迹象。伊柳埃卡先生说，中国是在“试图让巴拿马吃苦头”。当被问及如何概括自己掌舵七年的经历时，巴斯克斯先生说：“波动。”他的继任者可以预期，还会有更多这样的波动。■

paragraphs.en: Correction (July 16th 2026): An earlier version of this story mistakenly said the pipeline would carry lng. It will carry lpg. Sorry.
paragraphs.zh: 更正（2026年7月16日）：本文早前版本误称该管道将输送液化天然气。它将输送液化石油气。谨致歉意。

paragraphs.en: Sign up to El Boletín, our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《El Boletín》，这是我们面向订户的拉丁美洲新闻通讯，帮助你理解塑造这个迷人而复杂地区的各种力量。
